Container.Name: Siftr
Container.Overview: Siftr is a self-hosted photo manager that helps you sift through your library with smart renaming, organizing, and deduplication. Exact and perceptual duplicate detection is powered by czkawka, and EXIF write-back uses exiftool—both bundled in the image, nothing else to install. Point the /data volume at where your photos live and open the web UI.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port the Siftr web interface is served on.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Working data: the SQLite database, generated thumbnails, and the .trash holding area for removed duplicates. Keep this on a fast share (e.g. cache/appdata).

Config.Name: Photo Library
Config.Description: Your photo library. Siftr reads, renames, organizes and writes EXIF here, so it must be read/write. Mapped inside /data so it can live on a large array share separate from App Data.

Config.Name: Trash Folder
Config.Description: Holding area for duplicates removed via 'Move to trash' (reversible deletion). By default it lives inside App Data; map it to a separate share to keep trashed files off your appdata or to recover them elsewhere.

Config.Name: Enable Similar-Image Dedup
Config.Description: Also run czkawka's perceptual (near-duplicate) image pass in addition to exact hash matching. Slower. Set to true to enable.

Config.Name: Similar-Image Preset
Config.Description: czkawka similarity sensitivity for the perceptual pass: Minimal, VeryLow, Low, Medium, High, or VeryHigh. Only used when similar-image dedup is enabled.

Config.Name: Scan On Startup
Config.Description: Scan the photo library for new/changed files when the container starts. Set to false to only scan on demand.

Config.Name: Scan Concurrency
Config.Description: How many photos to hash and thumbnail in parallel during a scan. Raise on powerful CPUs, lower to reduce load.

Config.Name: Thumbnail Size
Config.Description: Edge length in pixels of generated grid thumbnails.
